devanagari: ऊषाअनिरुद्ध समागमः अनिरुद्धस्य बंधनं च । श्रीराजोवाच । बाणस्य तनयां ऊषां उपयेमे यदूत्तमः । तत्र युद्धमभूद् घोरं हरिशङ्करयोर्महत् । एतत्...
english_devnagari: ūṣāaniruddha samāgamaḥ aniruddhasya baṃdhanaṃ caśrīrājovācabāṇasya tanayāṃ ūṣāṃ upayeme yadūttamaḥtatra yuddhamabhūd ghoraṃ hariśaṅkarayormahatetatsarvaṃ mahāyogin samākhyātuṃ tvamarhasi
translation:
The King Parīkṣit submitted:
The best of Yadus (Aniruddha) married Uṣā, the daughter of Bāṇa. On this occasion a terrible and very great combat took place between Lord Hari and god Śaṅkara. O great yogin! May you be pleased to describe to me in details this episode.
[The Vrindāvan edition records the following verse from the Text of VT. and other commentators of Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava school.]
1(A). Bāṇa was the eldest out of the hundred sons of the noble-souled Bali. Being endowed with a thousand arms, he pleased god Śiva by playing on a musical instrument while the latter performed his Tāṇḍava dance.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । बाणः पुत्रशतज्येष्ठो बलेरासीन् महात्मनः । येन वामनरूपाय हरयेऽदायि मेदिनी ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācabāṇaḥ putraśatajyeṣṭho balerāsīn mahātmanaḥyena vāmanarūpāya haraye'dāyi medinī
translation:
Śrī Śuka narrated:
Bāṇa was the eldest of the hundred sons of the great- souled Bali who donated in charity the whole of the earth to to Lord Hari who presented himself before him in the form of Vāmana (a dwarf, a Brāhmaṇa boy).

devanagari: तस्यौरसः सुतो बानः शिवभक्तिरतः सदा । मान्यो वदान्यो धीमांश्च सत्यसन्धो दृढव्रतः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyaurasaḥ suto bānaḥ śivabhaktirataḥ sadāmānyo vadānyo dhīmāṃśca satyasandho dṛḍhavrataḥ
translation: Balī’s own son Bāṇa was always engaged in devotion to god Śiva. He was respectable, generous, intelligent, true of word and firm in observance of vows.

devanagari: शोणिताख्ये पुरे रम्ये स राज्यं अकरोत्पुरा । तस्य शम्भोः प्रसादेन किङ्करा इव तेऽमराः । सहस्रबाहुर्वाद्येन ताण्डवेऽतोषयन्मृडम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śoṇitākhye pure ramye sa rājyaṃ akarotpurātasya śambhoḥ prasādena kiṅkarā iva te'marāḥsahasrabāhurvādyena tāṇḍave'toṣayanmṛḍam
translation: Formerly, he used to rule over his kingdom from the beautiful city of Śoṇitapura[1] as its capital. By the grace of god Śiva even those celestials (the sworn enemies of Asuras) behaved with him as his servants. Endowed with a thousand arms as he was, he pleased god Śiva (the world-delighter) by playing upon musical instruments while god Śiva performed his Tāṇḍava[2] dance.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Assames claim Tejapur as the Śoṇitapur of Bāṇa. But the route followed by Kṛṣṇa in his expedition against Bāṇa and its siege etc. (as given in Harivaṃśa), show that Śoṇitapur in Kumaun on the back of Kedār-gaṅgā about 6 miles from Uṣāmaṭha near Gupta Kāśi has better claims to be this Śoṇitapur-GDAM p. 189.

Footnote [2]:
According to Bharata, the sage Taṇḍu was its initiator. But later as Śabdārthacintāmāṇi notes it came to be applied to all male dances and dancing by women was called lāsya. It implies violent type of dance in which Rudra indulges at the end of yugas. The violent effect of this dance is expressed in the 1st verse (MaṅgalaJ of Matsya Purāṇa. as follows:
pracaṇḍa-tāṇḍavāṭope praksiptā vena dig-gajāḥ /
bhavantu vighna-bhaṅgāya bhavasya caraṇāmbujāḥ //
  —Matṣya-purāṇa 1.1

devanagari: भगवान् सर्वभूतेशः शरण्यो भक्तवत्सलः । वरेण छन्दयामास स तं वव्रे पुराधिपम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavān sarvabhūteśaḥ śaraṇyo bhaktavatsalaḥvareṇa chandayāmāsa sa taṃ vavre purādhipam
translation: The glorious god Śiva, the Lord of all beings, worthy of seeking shelter, who is affectionate to his votaries, asked Bāṇa to seek any boon. Bāṇa begged of him to be the guardian deity of his capital.

devanagari: स एकदाऽह गिरिशं पार्श्वस्थं वीर्यदुर्मदः । किरीटेनार्कवर्णेन संस्पृशन् तत्पदाम्बुजम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ekadā'ha giriśaṃ pārśvasthaṃ vīryadurmadaḥkirīṭenārkavarṇena saṃspṛśan tatpadāmbujam
translation: On one occasion, Bāṇa who was madly elated with his prowess, touched with his crown brilliant like the sun, the lotus-feet of god Śiva who (as the presiding deity of his capital) was near to him and said:

devanagari: नमस्ये त्वां महादेव लोकानां गुरुमीश्वरम् । पुंसां अपूर्णकामानां कामपूरामराङ्घ्रिपम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: namasye tvāṃ mahādeva lokānāṃ gurumīśvarampuṃsāṃ apūrṇakāmānāṃ kāmapūrāmarāṅghripam
translation: “I bow to you, O great God, the preceptor and the highest Ruler of the world, the Wish-yielding tree fulfilling the desires of persons whose longings are not satisfied.

devanagari: दोःसहस्रं त्वया दत्तं परं भाराय मेऽभवत् । त्रिलोक्यां प्रतियोद्धारं न लभे त्वदृते समम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: doḥsahasraṃ tvayā dattaṃ paraṃ bhārāya me'bhavattrilokyāṃ pratiyoddhāraṃ na labhe tvadṛte samam
translation: The (boon of) thousand arms conferred by you have resulted into a burden to me; for I do not find an equal combatant (against me in the three worlds excepting you).

devanagari: कण्डूत्या निभृतैर्दोर्भिः युयुत्सुर्दिग्गजानहम् । आद्यायां चूर्णयन्नद्रीन् भीतास्तेऽपि प्रदुद्रुवुः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṇḍūtyā nibhṛtairdorbhiḥ yuyutsurdiggajānahamādyāyāṃ cūrṇayannadrīn bhītāste'pi pradudruvuḥ
translation: With my arms itching for a fight and being impelled by eagerness to combat, I marched against the elephants (supporting the world) at the end of cardinal points, crushing down the mountains on the way. But being terrified, the elephants hastily took to flight, O Ancient-most Person”.

devanagari: तत् श्रुत्वा भगवान् क्रुद्धः केतुस्ते भज्यते यदा । त्वद्दर्पघ्नं भवेन्मूढ संयुगं मत्समेन ते ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tat śrutvā bhagavān kruddhaḥ ketuste bhajyate yadātvaddarpaghnaṃ bhavenmūḍha saṃyugaṃ matsamena te
translation: Hearing this (arrogant speech), the glorious god Śiva got enraged and told him, “When your flag-staff (with the peacock ensign) shall be broken down automatically, your combat with some one equal to me will take place and it will humiliate (break down) your pride, O stupid fellow”.

devanagari: इत्युक्तः कुमतिर्हृष्टः स्वगृहं प्राविशन्नृप । प्रतीक्षन् गिरिशादेशं स्ववीर्यनशनं कुधीः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktaḥ kumatirhṛṣṭaḥ svagṛhaṃ prāviśannṛpapratīkṣan giriśādeśaṃ svavīryanaśanaṃ kudhīḥ
translation: Thus snubbed, the Asura with perverse mentality became delighted and repaired to his place. The crooked- minded fellow was waiting for the fulfilment of Śiva’s augery which indicated a crushing blow to his prowess.

devanagari: तस्योषा नाम दुहिता स्वप्ने प्राद्युम्निना रतिम् । कन्यालभत कान्तेन प्राग् अदृष्टश्रुतेन सा ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyoṣā nāma duhitā svapne prādyumninā ratimkanyālabhata kāntena prāg adṛṣṭaśrutena sā
translation: He had a daughter called Uṣā. Virgin as she was, in dream, she enjoyed sexual pleasure with Aniruddha, the son of Pradyumna as her beloved consort whom she had never seen or heard of.

devanagari: सा तत्र तमपश्यन्ती क्वासि कान्तेति वादिनी । सखीनां मध्य उत्तस्थौ विह्वला व्रीडिता भृशम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sā tatra tamapaśyantī kvāsi kānteti vādinīsakhīnāṃ madhya uttasthau vihvalā vrīḍitā bhṛśam
translation: Not finding him there near her (after opening her eyes), she got up in a bewildered state, exclaiming “Where are you my beloved lord?”, in the midst of her girl-companions and was exceedingly abashed.

devanagari: बाणस्य मंत्री कुम्भाण्डः चित्रलेखा च तत्सुता । सख्यपृच्छत् सखीं ऊषां कौतूहलसमन्विता ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: bāṇasya maṃtrī kumbhāṇḍaḥ citralekhā ca tatsutāsakhyapṛcchat sakhīṃ ūṣāṃ kautūhalasamanvitā
translation: Bāṇa had a minister called Kumbhāṇḍa. His daughter Citralekhā was her bosom-friend. Out of great curiosity, she enquired her friend Uṣā:

devanagari: कं त्वं मृगयसे सुभ्रु कीदृशस्ते मनोरथः । हस्तग्राहं न तेऽद्यापि राजपुत्र्युपलक्षये ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṃ tvaṃ mṛgayase subhru kīdṛśaste manorathaḥhastagrāhaṃ na te'dyāpi rājaputryupalakṣaye
translation: “Whom are you searching for, O friend with beautiful eyebrows? what is the nature of your desire? As far as I have observed, as yet there has been no suitor to your hand, O princess.”

devanagari: दृष्टः कश्चिन्नरः स्वप्ने श्यामः कमललोचनः । पीतवासा बृहद् बाहुः योषितां हृदयंगमः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭaḥ kaścinnaraḥ svapne śyāmaḥ kamalalocanaḥpītavāsā bṛhad bāhuḥ yoṣitāṃ hṛdayaṃgamaḥ
translation:
Uṣā narrated:
“In my dream I saw an unknown man of dark complexion, lotus-eyed, endowed with (knee-) long arms, wearing a yellow (silken) garment, captivating the hearts of women (capable of enjoying them).

devanagari: तमहं मृगये कान्तं पाययित्वाधरं मधु । क्वापि यातः स्पृहयतीं क्षिप्त्वा मां वृजिनार्णवे ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tamahaṃ mṛgaye kāntaṃ pāyayitvādharaṃ madhukvāpi yātaḥ spṛhayatīṃ kṣiptvā māṃ vṛjinārṇave
translation: I am seeking after him as my beloved Lord who having allowed me to drink the honey of his lips, has gone (to some unknown place) throwing me into the sea of misery even though I am still thirsty (after him).”

devanagari: चित्रलेखोवाच । व्यसनं तेऽपकर्षामि त्रिलोक्यां यदि भाव्यते । तं आनेष्ये वरं यस्ते मनोहर्ता तमादिश ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: citralekhovācavyasanaṃ te'pakarṣāmi trilokyāṃ yadi bhāvyatetaṃ āneṣye varaṃ yaste manohartā tamādiśa
translation:
Citralekhā said:
“I shall remove (the cause of) your grief. If he who captivated your heart exists somewhere in the three worlds, I shall bring that man. Please tell me who it is.”

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वा देवगन्धर्व सिद्धचारणपन्नगान् । दैत्यविद्याधरान् यक्षान् मनुजांश्च यथालिखत् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvā devagandharva siddhacāraṇapannagāndaityavidyādharān yakṣān manujāṃśca yathālikhat
translation: Saying thus she drew graphic portraits (of elite youths) of gods, Gandharvas, Siddhas, Cāraṇas, serpents, Daityas, Vidyādharas, Yakṣas and human princes.

devanagari: मनुजेषु च सा वृष्नीन् शूरं आनकदुन्दुभिम् । व्यलिखद् रामकृष्णौ च प्रद्युम्नं वीक्ष्य लज्जिता ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: manujeṣu ca sā vṛṣnīn śūraṃ ānakadundubhimvyalikhad rāmakṛṣṇau ca pradyumnaṃ vīkṣya lajjitā
translation: Out of human beings, she drew the portrait of VṛṣṇiṆ’s: Ugrasena, Vasudeva, Balarāma, Kṛṣṇa. When she saw Pradyumna’s portrait, she felt shy.

devanagari: अनिरुद्धं विलिखितं वीक्ष्योषावाङ्मुखी ह्रिया । सोऽसावसाविति प्राह स्मयमाना महीपते ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: aniruddhaṃ vilikhitaṃ vīkṣyoṣāvāṅmukhī hriyāso'sāvasāviti prāha smayamānā mahīpate
translation: Seeing the carefully painted portrait of Aniruddha she looked down in shyness and exclaimed smilingly, “It is he. It is he.” O Ruler of the earth.

devanagari: चित्रलेखा तमाज्ञाय पौत्रं कृष्णस्य योगिनी । ययौ विहायसा राजन् द्वारकां कृष्णपालिताम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: citralekhā tamājñāya pautraṃ kṛṣṇasya yoginīyayau vihāyasā rājan dvārakāṃ kṛṣṇapālitām
translation: Citralekhā who was endowed with yogic powers knew that he was the grand-son of Kṛṣṇa and through the air, she went to Dvārakā under the protection of Kṛṣṇa, O King.

devanagari: तत्र सुप्तं सुपर्यङ्के प्राद्युम्निं योगमास्थिता । गृहीत्वा शोणितपुरं सख्यै प्रियमदर्शयत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra suptaṃ suparyaṅke prādyumniṃ yogamāsthitāgṛhītvā śoṇitapuraṃ sakhyai priyamadarśayat
translation: Resorting to her yogic powers, she bore Aniruddha, the son of Pradyumna, who was sleeping on an excellent bed and brought him to Śoṇitapura and presented her beloved to her friend, Uṣā.

devanagari: सा च तं सुन्दरवरं विलोक्य मुदितानना । दुष्प्रेक्ष्ये स्वगृहे पुम्भी रेमे प्राद्युम्निना समम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: sā ca taṃ sundaravaraṃ vilokya muditānanāduṣprekṣye svagṛhe pumbhī reme prādyumninā samam
translation: Beholding the most beautiful of beautiful persons, she was extremely rejoiced and in her private apartment which was inaccessible to the eyes of males, she enjoyed herself with the son of Pradyumna.

devanagari: परार्ध्यवासःस्रग्गन्ध धूपदीपासनादिभिः । पानभोजन भक्ष्यैश्च वाक्यैः शुश्रूषणार्चितः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: parārdhyavāsaḥsraggandha dhūpadīpāsanādibhiḥpānabhojana bhakṣyaiśca vākyaiḥ śuśrūṣaṇārcitaḥ
translation: He was respectfully received with excellent garments, wreaths of flowers, perfumes, sweet incense, lights, high seat, with drinks, foods and other eatables, with sweet conversations and personal service.

devanagari: गूढः कन्यापुरे शश्वत् प्रवृद्धस्नेहया तया । नाहर्गणान् स बुबुधे ऊषयापहृतेन्द्रियः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: gūḍhaḥ kanyāpure śaśvat pravṛddhasnehayā tayānāhargaṇān sa bubudhe ūṣayāpahṛtendriyaḥ
translation: Aniruḍdha who remained concealed in the princess’s apartment, got his senses captivated by Uṣā whose love for him was ever increasing and he did not know how many days rolled away.

devanagari: तां तथा यदुवीरेण भुज्यमानां हतव्रताम् । हेतुभिर्लक्षयाञ्चक्रुः आप्रीतां दुरवच्छदैः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ tathā yaduvīreṇa bhujyamānāṃ hatavratāmhetubhirlakṣayāñcakruḥ āprītāṃ duravacchadaiḥ
translation: As she was thus enjoyed by the Yādava hero and lost her virginity, the attendants observed through marks which could be hardly concealed, that she was enjoying specially joyous life.

devanagari: भटा आवेदयाञ्चक्रू राजंस्ते दुहितुर्वयम् । विचेष्टितं लक्षयाम कन्यायाः कुलदूषणम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhaṭā āvedayāñcakrū rājaṃste duhiturvayamviceṣṭitaṃ lakṣayāma kanyāyāḥ kuladūṣaṇam
translation: The servants reported to Bāṇa, “O King, from the behaviour of your daughter, we mark that she is casting a stain on your family.

devanagari: अनपायिभिरस्माभिः गुप्तायाश्च गृहे प्रभो । कन्याया दूषणं पुम्भिः दुष्प्रेक्ष्याया न विद्महे ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: anapāyibhirasmābhiḥ guptāyāśca gṛhe prabhokanyāyā dūṣaṇaṃ pumbhiḥ duṣprekṣyāyā na vidmahe
translation: Though we were cautiously guarding her, we do not know how such pollution took place in the apartment of your virgin. daughter who was closely guarded and was beyond the reach of male eyes.”

devanagari: ततः प्रव्यथितो बाणो दुहितुः श्रुतदूषणः । त्वरितः कन्यकागारं प्राप्तोऽद्राक्षीद् यदूद्वहम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ pravyathito bāṇo duhituḥ śrutadūṣaṇaḥtvaritaḥ kanyakāgāraṃ prāpto'drākṣīd yadūdvaham
translation: Thereupon Bāṇa who was sorely distressed to hear of his daughter’s pollution, hastened immediately to the appartment of his daughter and saw there the best of the Yadus.

devanagari: कामात्मजं तं भुवनैकसुन्दरं । श्यामं पिशङ्गाम्बरमम्बुजेक्षणम् । बृहद्भुजं कुण्डलकुन्तलत्विषा । स्मितावलोकेन च मण्डिताननम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: kāmātmajaṃ taṃ bhuvanaikasundaraṃśyāmaṃ piśaṅgāmbaramambujekṣaṇambṛhadbhujaṃ kuṇḍalakuntalatviṣāsmitāvalokena ca maṇḍitānanam
translation: He saw Aniruddha who was the son of Kāma (Pradyumna), the most beautiful person in the world, dark in complexion, wearing a yellow garment, of lotus eyes, long and mighty arms and with his countenance brightened with the lustre of his ear-rings, and locks of hair and was beaming with smiling looks.

devanagari: दीव्यन्तमक्षैः प्रिययाभिनृम्णया । तदङ्गसङ्गस्तनकुङ्कुमस्रजम् । बाह्वोर्दधानं मधुमल्लिकाश्रितां । तस्याग्र आसीनमवेक्ष्य विस्मितः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: dīvyantamakṣaiḥ priyayābhinṛmṇayātadaṅgasaṅgastanakuṅkumasrajambāhvordadhānaṃ madhumallikāśritāṃtasyāgra āsīnamavekṣya vismitaḥ
translation: Bāṇa was shocked to see Aniruḍdha playing at dice with his beloved daughter who on her part adorned herself with festle ornaments—and wearing between his arms a garland of Jasmine flowers, special to the season of spring, tinged with the saffron on her bosom during his contacts with her person seated in front of her,

devanagari: स तं प्रविष्टं वृतमाततायिभिः । भटैरनीकैरवलोक्य माधवः । उद्यम्य मौर्वं परिघं व्यवस्थितो । यथान्तको दण्डधरो जिघांसया ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa taṃ praviṣṭaṃ vṛtamātatāyibhiḥbhaṭairanīkairavalokya mādhavaḥudyamya maurvaṃ parighaṃ vyavasthitoyathāntako daṇḍadharo jighāṃsayā
translation: Seeing Bāṇa enter surrounded with fierce looking soldiers, Aniruḍdha, the descendent of Madhu, took up an iron bludgeon and took position like Yama, the wielḍer of the rod determined to finish with any body that may approach him.

devanagari: जिघृक्षया तान् परितः प्रसर्पतः । शुनो यथा शूकरयूथपोऽहनत् । ते हन्यमाना भवनाद् विनिर्गता । निर्भिन्नमूर्धोरुभुजाः प्रदुद्रुवुः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: jighṛkṣayā tān paritaḥ prasarpataḥśuno yathā śūkarayūthapo'hanatte hanyamānā bhavanād vinirgatānirbhinnamūrdhorubhujāḥ pradudruvuḥ
translation: Like a great boar that kicks away the attacking dogs, he struck them down who attacked him on all sides with the intention of arresting him. Beaten thus by him, they ran out of Uṣā’s palace with their heads, thighs and arms broken.

devanagari: तं नागपाशैर्बलिनन्दनो बली । घ्नन्तं स्वसैन्यं कुपितो बबन्ध ह । ऊषा भृशं शोकविषादविह्वला । बद्धं निशम्याश्रुकलाक्ष्यरौदिषीत् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ nāgapāśairbalinandano balīghnantaṃ svasainyaṃ kupito babandha haūṣā bhṛśaṃ śokaviṣādavihvalābaddhaṃ niśamyāśrukalākṣyaraudiṣīt
translation: The powerful son of Bali got angry with Aniruddha who was striking down his soldiers and he bound him down with Nāgapāśa (serpent-like cords).

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । अनिरुद्धबन्धो नाम द्विषष्टितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃsaṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdheaniruddhabandho nāma dviṣaṣṭitamo'dhyāyaḥ
